title: Police Station Rep Near Me in Kent: How to Get Free Legal Advice Fast

metaDescription: Need a police station rep near you in Kent? How the duty solicitor scheme works, all Kent custody suites, and how to get free legal advice fast. Call 01732 247427.
featuredImageAlt: Police station representation available across Kent custody suites
contentHtml:
Introduction

If you search for a police station rep near me in Kent, you need someone who can attend quickly — whether you are in custody now or have a booked voluntary interview. This guide explains how to find representation across Kent, which custody suites operate 24 hours, and how free legal advice works.

Police Station Agent is a private defence website operated by Robert Cashman — NOT Kent Police. Legal services are delivered through Tuckers Solicitors LLP (SRA ID: 127795).

Key takeaways

  • Searching police station rep near me in Kent should lead you to the duty solicitor scheme — free advice for most interviews.
  • Kent has multiple 24-hour custody suites including Medway, North Kent (Gravesend), Tonbridge, Canterbury, and Folkestone.
  • Voluntary interviews take place at local stations such as Maidstone, Sevenoaks, Dartford, and Swanley.
  • Ask for Robert Cashman, Tuckers Duty Solicitor — qualified solicitor cover across Kent.

1) How to get a police station rep in Kent quickly

If you are in current custody, tell the custody officer you want legal advice and ask for Robert Cashman, Tuckers Duty Solicitor. Immediate family may also contact us on your behalf — see immediate family instruction.

For a booked voluntary interview, call us as soon as you receive the date. Do not wait until the day of the interview.

Telephone 01732 247427 · Text 07535 494446 · Someone in custody now

2) Kent 24-hour custody suites

Main Kent custody facilities include:

  • Medway (Gillingham) — serving Medway towns
  • North Kent (Gravesend, Thames Way) — north Kent including Dartford and Gravesend
  • Tonbridge — west Kent 24-hour suite
  • Canterbury — east Kent
  • Folkestone — coastal east Kent

Operational routing can vary. A person arrested in one area may be detained at another suite depending on capacity. See Kent police station reps hub and all locations.

3) Voluntary interview stations

Many Kent towns host voluntary interviews without custody facilities — including Maidstone (custody closed; voluntary interviews at Palace Avenue), Sevenoaks, Dartford, Swanley, and Tunbridge Wells. Free legal advice applies equally to voluntary interviews.

See Maidstone voluntary interviews and Sevenoaks voluntary interviews.

4) Qualified solicitor — not a call centre

When searching online you may find call-centre style agencies. Robert Cashman is a qualified duty solicitor practising through Tuckers Solicitors LLP (SRA ID 127795) — regulated, insured, and independent of the police.

See qualified duty solicitor vs police station rep.

5) Town and area guides

  • North Kent (Gravesend) custody
  • Tonbridge custody and interviews
  • Medway custody
  • Canterbury custody
  • Folkestone custody

6) What information helps us attend

Full name, date of birth, which station or custody suite, custody record number if known, and interview date/time for voluntary attendances. See custody record and DSCC references.

7) The DSCC and how attendance is arranged

When a detainee requests legal advice in custody, custody staff contact the Defence Solicitor Call Centre (DSCC). The DSCC allocates a duty solicitor firm to attend. You may request a named duty solicitor — ask for Robert Cashman, Tuckers Duty Solicitor. For voluntary interviews, you contact us directly before the date.

Provide the detainee's full name, date of birth, station or custody suite, custody record number if known, and offence type if known. This helps us attend promptly and prepared.

8) Response times — custody vs voluntary

Current custody is urgent. Call immediately on 01732 247427. We prioritise live custody matters. Voluntary interviews should be booked as soon as you receive the date — same-day instruction is possible but early instruction allows disclosure requests and proper preparation.

9) Dartford, Swanley and north Kent voluntary stations

North Kent arrests often route to the Gravesend custody suite at Thames Way, Northfleet. Voluntary interviews may take place at Dartford, Gravesend, or other local stations. West Kent voluntary interviews may be at Sevenoaks or Tonbridge. East Kent may use Canterbury or Folkestone for either custody or voluntary attendances.

See North Kent Gravesend custody, Dartford cover, and Swanley cover.

10) After the police station visit

Outcomes may include charge, release under investigation, police bail, or no further action. Your solicitor's attendance notes record what happened. Keep those details safe. See no further action explained and RUI in plain English.

11) Choosing a qualified solicitor over a call-centre agency

Online searches for "police station rep near me" may show lead-generation websites that are not law firms. Always confirm which regulated firm is attending. Robert Cashman practises through Tuckers Solicitors LLP (SRA ID 127795). Ask for him by name when you call or when speaking to custody staff.

See qualified duty solicitor vs police station rep.

12) Medway, Canterbury and east Kent quick reference

Medway (Gillingham) — main custody for Medway towns. Canterbury — east Kent including Herne Bay and Whitstable. Folkestone — coastal east Kent. Maidstone — voluntary interviews only. See our town guides for each area linked from all locations.

13) What to expect when your solicitor attends

Your solicitor will usually speak to the investigating officer to request disclosure, then consult with you in private before any interview. They remain with you during interview and take notes of the outcome. This applies whether you are in custody at Gravesend, Tonbridge, Medway, Canterbury, or Folkestone, or attending a voluntary interview at Maidstone, Sevenoaks, or Dartford.

See when to ask for a solicitor, arrange a solicitor in custody, and police station representation service.

14) North Kent and Gravesend — regular attendance

North Kent (Gravesend) custody at Thames Way, Northfleet is one of the county's busiest 24-hour suites. Detainees from Dartford, Gravesend, Swanscombe, and wider north Kent are often brought there. Robert Cashman provides extended-hours cover across Kent with regular attendance at North Kent and Tonbridge among other stations.

See North Kent Gravesend custody legal advice for a detailed station guide.

15) Tonbridge and west Kent custody

Tonbridge operates the main west Kent 24-hour custody suite. Arrests from Sevenoaks, Tunbridge Wells, and west Kent villages may route there. Voluntary interviews in west Kent may be at Sevenoaks, Tonbridge, or Tunbridge Wells. See Tonbridge custody and interviews.

16) Medway custody for Medway towns

Medway (Gillingham) custody serves Rochester, Chatham, Gillingham, Strood, and wider Medway. See Medway custody legal advice and Medway cover. For any Kent station, call 01732 247427 and ask for Robert Cashman, Tuckers Duty Solicitor.

17) Summary — finding a rep fast in Kent

For current custody call 01732 247427 immediately. For voluntary interviews call as soon as you receive the date. Ask for Robert Cashman, Tuckers Duty Solicitor. Free legal advice applies to most interviews at every Kent custody suite and voluntary interview station.

See is advice free?, Kent police station reps hub, and when to ask for a solicitor.

Need legal advice at a Kent police station?

Call 01732 247427 for current custody or a booked voluntary interview. If you cannot call, text 07535 494446.

Ask for Robert Cashman, Tuckers Duty Solicitor — the DSCC have our details. Legal services are provided by Tuckers Solicitors LLP (SRA ID: 127795).

Contact Police Station Agent · Someone in custody now · Free police station advice in Kent

Conclusion

For a police station rep near you in Kent, call 01732 247427 now. Ask for Robert Cashman, Tuckers Duty Solicitor. Free legal advice is available for most police station interviews.

faq.q: How do I find a police station rep near me in Kent?
faq.a: Call 01732 247427 for current custody or a booked voluntary interview. Ask for Robert Cashman, Tuckers Duty Solicitor. Legal advice is free for most interviews.

faq.q: Which Kent towns do you cover?
faq.a: We attend custody suites and voluntary interviews across Kent including Medway, Gravesend, Tonbridge, Canterbury, Folkestone, Maidstone, Sevenoaks, and Dartford, subject to availability.

faq.q: Is a police station rep the same as a duty solicitor?
faq.a: A duty solicitor is a qualified solicitor. Some attendances are by accredited representatives. Robert Cashman is a qualified solicitor and duty solicitor.

faq.q: Can family find a rep for someone in custody?
faq.a: Immediate family may contact us to help arrange a solicitor when someone is in current custody, subject to the detainee confirming they want advice.

author: Robert Cashman
